description: Red Lion Crimson 3.0 is open. A sugar mill is commissioning SCADA for a multiple-effect evaporation station that concentrates clarified cane juice into thick syrup. You are the process instrumentation engineer responsible for Evaporation Train A. Your task is to configure the Evaporation Train A tags in Crimson per ICUMSA/BMA standards. Both reference documents — the tag register CSV and the ICUMSA/BMA standards document — are in the CrimsonTasks folder on the Desktop. The tag register lists tags for both Train-A (active) and Train-B (scheduled for next season). Configure ONLY the active Train-A tags. Do NOT configure any Train-B tags. Each active tag must be fully configured with the correct data type, engineering range, unit label, description, and alarm thresholds. IMPORTANT: There are two intentional discrepancies between the tag register CSV and the standards document regarding alarm limits. The ICUMSA/BMA standards document is authoritative and reflects the latest revisions. Where conflicts exist, you MUST use the values from the standards document. Save the project as 'sugar_evaporation.c3' in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\. Login credentials: Docker / GymAnything123!

metadata.occupation: Separating, Filtering, Clarifying, Precipitating, and Still Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
